candidates.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
candidates.note: Lannister side; Tyrion's retrospective attributes the destruction campaign directly to Tywin (line 367).

candidates.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
candidates.note: House Reyne destroyed root and branch (line 367); battle subtype passes harm-gate (destruction).

candidates.quote: He had extinguished the proud Reynes of Castamere and the ancient Tarbecks of Tarbeck Hall root and branch when he was still half a boy.
candidates.note: House Tarbeck destroyed root and branch (line 367).

candidates.quote: When Tarbeck Hall came crashing down on Lady Ellyn, that scheming bitch, Tyg claimed he smiled then.
candidates.note: Ellyn (Reyne by birth, Lady Tarbeck by marriage) named directly as the hall is destroyed on her (line 273).

candidates.quote: Sandog tore Viserys's battleaxe from the wood where the prince had buried it and split Ser Amaury's head and helm in half from crest to gorget.
candidates.note: Kills Ser Amaury Peake at the drawbridge of Maegor's Holdfast (line 263, spelled 'Sandog' in this OCR pass). event.incident node_type bars VICTIM_IN for Amaury Peake — see R3-06 (FIGHTS_IN) instead.

candidates.quote: Yet Peake fought on with stubborn valor, and near the end, for half a heartbeat, the gods gave him his chance
candidates.note: Leads the guard assault on the holdfast and is killed there (line 259); routed to FIGHTS_IN not VICTIM_IN — event.incident harm-gate excludes VICTIM_IN despite the death.

candidates.quote: “If you are come to take my lady wife, ser, turn and go,” the young prince said, “for you shall not pass whilst I still stand.”
candidates.note: Personally blocks the drawbridge with a battle-axe to defend Larra Rogare (line 217).

candidates.quote: | commanded Ser Amaury to be gone, and he refused.
candidates.note: King commands the holdfast's defiance during the secret siege (line 271; '|' for 'I' is an OCR artifact, kept verbatim).

candidates.quote: Manacled at ankle with heavy chains, his face so bruised and swollen as to be unrecognizable, Lord Thaddeus did not move at first, until Lord Graceford pricked him with the point of his dagger
candidates.note: Central accused figure whose tortured, later-disavowed confession nearly topples the regency (line 49). VICTIM_IN not available under event.incident harm-gate despite the torture — flagged.

candidates.quote: The army Lord Alyn was to carry to the Vale would be commanded by Lady Larra’s brother Moredo Rogare, Lord Rowan announced.
candidates.note: Commands the Vale relief army during the Gilded Falcon/Mad Heir succession crisis, the same crisis year as the secret siege (line 141).

candidates.quote: The rest of the Red Keep was in the hands of Ser Marston Waters and his Kingsguard, whilst beyond the castle walls Ser Lucas Leygood and his gold cloaks kept a firm grip on King’s Landing.
candidates.note: New Hand commands the besieging force during the eighteen-day secret siege of Maegor's Holdfast (line 267).

candidates.quote: Daenerys Targaryen eased herself onto the chair’s plush velvet seat, and Hizdahr zo Loraq went to his knees, unlaced her sandals, and washed her feet
candidates.note: Bride at the Temple of the Graces ceremony (line 347); matches existing graph convention (bride/groom = AGENT_IN on ceremony hubs, cf. wedding-ceremony-at-the-great-sept-of-baelor).

candidates.quote: Four hours later, they emerged again as man and wife, bound together wrist and ankle with chains of yellow gold.
candidates.note: Groom (line 351).

candidates.quote: Hand in hand, they followed the Green Grace inside the temple, where the air was thick with incense and the gods of Ghis stood cloaked in shadows in their alcoves.
candidates.note: The Green Grace leads the couple into the temple rite (line 349); Galazza is the named Green Grace throughout this arc.

candidates.quote: “Magnificence, you do not understand,” protested Reznak. “The washing of the feet is hallowed by tradition.
candidates.note: Organizes/negotiates the wedding customs and preparations with Daenerys (line 127) — administrative, non-combat.

candidates.quote: I think we should attack from three sides. Grey Worm, your Unsullied shall strike at them from right and left, while my kos lead my horse in wedge for a thrust through their center.
candidates.note: Targaryen side, overall commander of the campaign (line 207).

candidates.quote: When she had commanded the Unsullied to choose officers from amongst themselves, Grey Worm had been their overwhelming choice for the highest rank.
candidates.note: Targaryen side; commands the Unsullied (line 39).

candidates.quote: Leading her van, Daario had given orders for the children to be taken down before Dany had to see them
candidates.note: Targaryen side; commands the Stormcrows in the van of the march (line 33).

candidates.quote: unlike Ser Jorah, Daario, Brown Ben, and her three bloodriders, the eunuch did not lead troops, plan battles, or give her counsel.
candidates.note: Targaryen side; text confirms Jorah leads troops and plans battles for Dany (line 55).

candidates.quote: unlike Ser Jorah, Daario, Brown Ben, and her three bloodriders, the eunuch did not lead troops, plan battles, or give her counsel.
candidates.note: Targaryen side; Second Sons captain, already fighting for Dany at this point in ASOS (pre-dates his later ADWD defection to Yunkai, see ben-plumm-defects-to-yunkai) (line 55).

candidates.quote: And Dany swept the lash down as hard as she could across the slaver’s face.
candidates.note: Personally strikes Kraznys and commands Drogon's fire (line 253).

candidates.quote: A lance of swirling dark flame took Kraznys full in the face.
candidates.note: Killed by Drogon's fire in the Plaza of Punishment (line 257).

candidates.quote: Drogon flew almost lazily at Kraznys, black wings beating.
candidates.note: The dragon is the direct executor of Kraznys's death (line 259).

candidates.quote: Jhogo’s whip coiled about his neck and cut off his shout.
candidates.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

candidates.quote: Another lost a hand to Rakharo’s arakh and rode off reeling and spurting blood.
candidates.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

candidates.quote: Aggo sat calmly notching arrows to his bowstring and sending them at tokars.
candidates.note: Bloodrider, fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

candidates.quote: Strong Belwas had his arakh out as well, and he spun it as he charged.
candidates.note: Fights in the plaza chaos (line 259).

candidates.quote: And all around them slavers ran and sobbed and begged and died, and the dusty air was filled with spears and fire.
candidates.note: Faction node; executes Dany's 'Slay the Good Masters' order (line 271), the mechanism of the betrayal/massacre.

candidates.quote: Prince Rhaegar carried her off and raped her,” Bran explained.
candidates.note: Abduction is a listed harm subtype; taken as fact of the in-world narrative regardless of the R+L=J theory question (which stays gated).

candidates.quote: Prince Rhaegar carried her off and raped her,” Bran explained.
candidates.note: The dominant in-world/realm framing (Bran's account; Robert's 'how many times do you think he raped your sister?' at agot-eddard-02.md:79 is the same claim from the other side). Node already carries rhaegar-targaryen SUSPECTED_OF abduction-of-lyanna (tier-2, deliberately hedged) — this AGENT_IN reifies the dominant accusatory narrative itself, not a graph assertion of settled fact; the opposing 'died for the woman he loved' counter-frame (agot-daenerys-08.md:187, already cited on the node) is the acknowledged counter-testimony. Flagging the overlap per dedup rule rather than silently duplicating.

candidates.quote: the Kingslayer descended on them and put them to flight.
candidates.note: Lannister side, commands the force that routs Vance and Piper (line 123).

candidates.quote: Lord Vance was slain.
candidates.note: Killed holding the pass (line 123).

candidates.quote: Lord Piper was falling back to join your brother and his other bannermen at Riverrun, with Jaime Lannister on his heels.
candidates.note: Riverlands side, survives and retreats (line 123).

candidates.quote: Edmure has commanded Lord Vance and Lord Piper to guard the pass below the Golden Tooth.
candidates.note: Riverlands side, orderer of the defense — did not personally fight this engagement (line 15).

candidates.quote: A man tried to pull me from the saddle. The Hound killed him, I think . . . his arm . . .
candidates.note: Nearly pulled from her horse by the mob (line 133); node_type is literally event.battle so VICTIM_IN passes the gate.

candidates.quote: The Hound killed him, I think . . . his arm . . .
candidates.note: Kills a rioter defending Sansa, cutting off his arm (line 133).

candidates.quote: They were traitors,” Joffrey squealed from the ground. “They called me names and attacked me!”
candidates.note: Attacked by the mob, precipitating Sandor's killing (line ~99).

candidates.quote: Bronn had a score of sellswords scattered through the crowd with orders to stop any trouble before it started.
candidates.note: Acting Hand organizing crowd-control ahead of the riot (line 59) — administrative, not personal combat.

candidates.quote: Bronn had a score of sellswords scattered through the crowd with orders to stop any trouble before it started.
candidates.note: Deploys sellswords into the crowd at Tyrion's order (line 59).

candidates.quote: It is,” Aegon replied eagerly. “If my aunt wants Meereen, she’s welcome to it. I will claim the Iron Throne by myself, with your swords and your allegiance.
candidates.note: Personally makes the sail-west decision (line 217).

candidates.quote: Prince Aegon,” said Tristan Rivers, “we are your men. Is this your wish, that we sail west instead of east?”
candidates.note: Puts the decisive question to Aegon on behalf of the council (line 215).

candidates.quote: The last to do so was Homeless Harry Strickland, blistered feet and all.
candidates.note: Captain-general; argued for caution but is the last to kneel and pledge, joining the decision organizationally (line 223). Overlaps with the existing harry-strickland OPPOSES edge on this node (he opposed the plan before acquiescing).

candidates.quote: Ride back to the Shy Maid and return with Lady Lemore and Ser Rolly. We’ll need Illyrio’s chests as well.
candidates.note: Organizes logistics immediately following the decision (line 229).

candidates.quote: Daemon was the Warrior himself that day. No man could stand before him. He broke Lord Arryn's van to pieces and slew the Knight of Ninestars and Wild Wyl Waynwood before coming up against Ser Gwayne Corbray of the Kingsguard.
candidates.note: Rebel/Blackfyre side, leads the rebel host and personally fights (line 703).

candidates.quote: The war was done when Daemon died.
candidates.note: Distinct second role, textually supported separately from his command role (line 705).

candidates.quote: He slew Aegon first, the elder of the twins, for he knew that Daemon would never leave the boy whilst warmth lingered in his body
candidates.note: Loyalist side (King's Hand); personally kills Aegon and Aemon Blackfyre with arrows from Weeping Ridge, turning the battle (line 703).

candidates.quote: He slew Aegon first, the elder of the twins, for he knew that Daemon would never leave the boy whilst warmth lingered in his body
candidates.note: Killed by Bloodraven's arrows (line 703).

candidates.quote: Young Aemon took up Blackfyre when the blade slipped from his dying father's fingers, so Bloodraven slew him, too, the younger of the twins.
candidates.note: Killed by Bloodraven immediately after his father and twin (line 703).

candidates.quote: the gift Bittersteel had given him upon the Redgrass Field.
candidates.note: Bittersteel puts out Bloodraven's eye in this battle (line 51, Dunk's recollection); text also has him 'turning the rout and leading his mad charge' afterward (line 705).

candidates.quote: he might have broken Maekar's left before Bloodraven could take the ridge.
candidates.note: Loyalist side, commands a wing ('Maekar's left') — his alias 'The Anvil' matches the 'hammer and the anvil' song reference on this node (line 707).

candidates.quote: Prince Baelor's hammerblow against the rebel rear, the Dornishmen all screaming as they filled the air with spears
candidates.note: Loyalist side, commands the Dornish contingent that strikes the rebel rear (line 705).

candidates.quote: You are to harry the Stony Shore, raiding the fishing villages and sinking any ships you chance to meet.
candidates.note: Orders the raid (line 399), does not personally execute it.

candidates.quote: I mean for you to strike the first blow, Theon. You shall take eight longships north—
candidates.note: Given nominal command of the raid party by Balon (line 395); acok-theon-03.md:53 notes the command is contested by his own men.

candidates.quote: Down to the pebbled beach they would go, to drown Benfred Tallhart in salt water.
candidates.note: Oversees/performs the ritual drowning of the captured Benfred Tallhart (line 39).

candidates.quote: Down to the pebbled beach they would go, to drown Benfred Tallhart in salt water.
candidates.note: Captured and drowned during the raid (line 39); his death is the node's stated mechanism for concealing the invasion's true size.

candidates.quote: Theon had assigned him the task of guarding the ships; otherwise men would have called it Dagmer’s victory, not his.
candidates.note: Co-commands the raiding party aboard Foamdrinker (line 65).

wiki_only.quote: Lord Roger Reyne, Ellyn's older brother, laughed when he read Tywin's edicts and advised his friends and vassals to do nothing.
wiki_only.note: Rebel side; not found named directly in book text searched (asos/affc), only in wiki-sourced node Origins.

wiki_only.quote: Lord Walderan Tarbeck hoped to convince Tytos to rescind Tywin's edicts and traveled to Casterly Rock, but was instead imprisoned by Tywin.
wiki_only.note: See flags — asos-jaime-06.md:91 book text attributes an imprisonment episode of 'Lord Tarbeck' to Tytos Lannister, not Tywin, conflicting with this wiki claim.

wiki_only.quote: In return, his wife Ellyn seized three Lannisters – two Lannisters of Lannisport, and Stafford Lannister, whose sister Joanna was betrothed to Tywin – and threatened them harm unless her husband was returned.
wiki_only.note: Taken hostage by Ellyn Tarbeck; not found named directly in the book chapters searched.

flags: reyne-tarbeck-revolt: asos-jaime-06.md:91 book text ('Tywin Lannister's own father Lord Tytos had once imprisoned an unruly bannerman, Lord Tarbeck. The redoubtable Lady Tarbeck responded by capturing three Lannisters...') attributes the Tarbeck-imprisonment/hostage episode to TYTOS Lannister, not Tywin, while the node's wiki-sourced Origins prose attributes the imprisonment to Tywin himself. Did not mint a tier-1 candidate for Walderan Tarbeck's imprisonment because of this book-vs-wiki conflict; routed to wiki_only (R3-W2) instead. Worth a fresh look at whether the node's Origins section has a Tytos/Tywin mixup.

lysene-spring (event.incident): this node is unusually harm-dense (torture, multiple killings, a poisoning conspiracy) but its node_type bars VICTIM_IN entirely per the harm-gate. Several genuinely victimized figures (Amaury Peake killed, Thaddeus Rowan tortured, Ser Amaury's men, the poisoned Gaemon Palehair) were routed to FIGHTS_IN/PARTICIPATES_IN or omitted rather than force-fit. Flagging for whoever reviews node-type assignments: this reads more like an event.incident that contains an embedded event.battle (the secret siege / drawbridge fight) — a possible future sub-beat split, not something this proposer should mint unilaterally.

war-for-the-dawn: yielded ZERO candidates. The node's own text is entirely Melisandre's prophecy discourse with Davos and Aemon (plus Stannis recounting a vision) — no dated battle, no named combatants or commanders on-page within this node. The actual fighting this prophecy refers to lives in separate battle nodes (e.g., battle-of-the-fist-of-the-first-men, referenced inside Stannis's vision quote). Did not force a WITNESS_IN for Stannis's fire-vision since it's a prophetic sending, not a direct-perception incident per the WITNESS_IN text-anchor gate.

abduction-of-lyanna: kept to exactly 2 candidates (Lyanna VICTIM_IN, Rhaegar AGENT_IN tier-2) per the strict-honesty instruction. Did NOT add Brandon Stark (his ride to King's Landing is already the causal CAUSES edge, not a role on the abduction itself) and did NOT add any R+L=J-theory-informed roles (out of scope, theories track is gated).

wedding-of-hizdahr-zo-loraq-and-daenerys-targaryen: did not add Barristan Selmy as ATTENDS — he voices skepticism about the marriage (adwd-daenerys-06.md) but the wedding-ceremony scene itself (adwd-daenerys-07.md:340-351) does not place him on-page at the temple; adding him would be inferential, not text-grounded. Also did not add Daario Naharis (ATTENDS or otherwise) — the existing SUB_BEAT_OF wedding-morning-daario-leaves-angrily edge plus the chapter text both establish he pointedly is NOT there.

targaryen-campaign-in-slavers-bay: used the same 'unlike Ser Jorah, Daario, Brown Ben, and her three bloodriders...' line (asos-daenerys-05.md:55) as grounding for both Jorah Mormont and Ben Plumm's COMMANDS_IN roles since it names both in one sentence establishing they lead troops/plan battles for Dany; flagging in case the reviewer wants only one of the two kept to avoid a same-quote-two-edges pattern.

battle-of-the-redgrass-field: this node is exceptionally quote-rich (Ser Eustace Osgrey's account to Dunk in tss-dunk-01.md) and yielded 8 candidates, at the top of the typical 2-8 range; trimmed Ser Gwayne Corbray (loyalist Kingsguard wounded in single combat with Daemon, tss-dunk-01.md:703) to keep the roster tight — a defensible 9th candidate if the reviewer wants it added back.

battle-in-the-hills-below-the-golden-tooth: did not add Ser Gregor Clegane — the node's Origins says Tywin 'dispatches' him to raid the Red Fork and he 'circumvents' the Tully forces guarding the Golden Tooth pass, meaning he explicitly avoids this particular engagement; adding him here would misattribute him to a fight he sidestepped.
